title: Intro to Political Science

jokes.text: So I go into the bathroom with Tom... And we both, you know... take 'em out. And I say, "Size more? Try size less."
jokes.setup: Pierce telling a bathroom story with Tom, building expectation
jokes.punchline: The pun "Size more? Try size less" - wordplay on 'size matters'
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce inappropriate oversharing with a decent pun. Character-appropriate but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: I wanna go to rehab and compare penises with famous people.
jokes.punchline: Absurd non-sequitur about rehab and celebrity comparison
jokes.explanation: Unexpected and economical absurdist line that catches the audience off-guard.

jokes.text: - Hello! - Oh, thank God! Yes! Oh, my God. - Thank you.
jokes.setup: Group is forced to study anthropology, clearly bored
jokes.punchline: Overwhelming relief when Dean interrupts their studying
jokes.explanation: Relatable student reaction to studying interruption. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: "Biden Time Talkin' 'Bout Teachin'" tour
jokes.punchline: Absurd punny name for Biden's tour
jokes.explanation: Clever political pun that feels authentic to both Biden and Community's style.

jokes.text: Mm, folksy, yet progressive. He does walk that line.
jokes.punchline: Deadpan political commentary on Biden's brand
jokes.explanation: Sharp political observation delivered with perfect Jeff Winger smugness. Economical and character-driven.

jokes.text: Eat that, city college and wash it down with a nice tall glass of suck it.
jokes.punchline: Escalating from polite competition to aggressive 'suck it'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dean Pelton escalation from professional to petty. The 'tall glass of suck it' construction is memorable.

jokes.text: Oh, Abed, will your reality ever come out on Blu-Ray so we can enjoy it?
jokes.punchline: Jeff making fun of Abed's observational skills with meta TV reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about Abed's character trait while referencing the show's own format. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: That explains the people I've noticed covertly surveilling the campus this week. Must be Secret Service.
jokes.setup: Everyone else is excited about Biden's visit
jokes.punchline: Abed casually mentions he's been tracking secret surveillance all week
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed - he notices everything but mentions it casually like it's obvious. Sets up his Secret Service subplot nicely.

jokes.text: We've been made.
jokes.setup: Jeff's sarcastic comment about Abed's observations
jokes.punchline: Abed's deadpan spy movie response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed response - treating Jeff's joke as if it were real surveillance drama. Maximum economy.

jokes.text: I mean, I could borrow my sister's Uncle Sam outfit. It's tailored for ladies but what else can I do on such short notice?
jokes.setup: Dean wondering what to wear for Biden
jokes.punchline: Dean's willingness to wear his 'sister's' feminine Uncle Sam outfit
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean Pelton cross-dressing setup with the 'sister' excuse. Character-driven cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Well, you can not dress up like Uncle Sam. Or admit you don't have a sister.
jokes.setup: Dean's sister's Uncle Sam outfit explanation
jokes.punchline: Jeff calling out the obvious fiction of Dean having a sister
jokes.explanation: Jeff's dry calling-out of Dean's transparent excuse. Good economy and character dynamic.

jokes.text: Improving Greendale takes more than ideas, Annie. It takes time, gasoline, matches.
jokes.setup: Annie wants to improve the school with ideas
jokes.punchline: Jeff suggesting arson as the solution
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation from improvement to destruction. Great structure building to 'matches' and very Jeff-like cynicism.

jokes.text: Well, news flash, Jeff. Some of us care about more than just fixing our hair and sculpting our abs.
jokes.setup: Annie defending her idealism against Jeff's cynicism
jokes.punchline: Calling out Jeff's vanity with specific details
jokes.explanation: Predictable but character-appropriate attack on Jeff's vanity. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Wow. You got me pegged.
jokes.punchline: Jeff's sarcastic non-denial
jokes.explanation: Jeff's typical deflection through sarcasm. Good economy but fairly standard.

jokes.text: Everyone wants you to shut up. And yet I won't. Case in point.
jokes.setup: Pierce making a point about democracy
jokes.punchline: Pierce acknowledging everyone hates him talking but continuing anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce self-awareness about being annoying while proving the point. Meta and character-specific.

jokes.text: And yes, this is a ladies Uncle Sam outfit. It's my sister's. It was last-minute. I had no choice.
jokes.setup: Visual of Dean in feminine Uncle Sam outfit
jokes.punchline: Dean pre-emptively defending his outfit choice
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier setup with visual payoff. Classic Dean costume comedy.

jokes.text: I wonder if that's the same sister that tweets me to ask if I think her brother's cute.
jokes.punchline: Jeff exposing Dean's 'sister' as Dean himself on Twitter
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the Dean's 'sister' bit revealing his catfishing. Character-perfect and memorable.

jokes.text: Real nice.
jokes.setup: Seeing Jeff threw away her campaign flyers
jokes.punchline: Annie's sarcastic underreaction to obvious sabotage
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat. Functional but not particularly inventive.

jokes.text: I'm more of a silverback gorilla with the claws a lion, the teeth of a shark, and the quiet dignity of a tortoise.
jokes.setup: Annie asking Jeff to help campaign
jokes.punchline: Jeff's ridiculous mixed-animal metaphor ending with 'quiet dignity of a tortoise'
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Jeff metaphor that escalates perfectly to the anti-climactic tortoise. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: I wanted ice cream, so I got in line...
jokes.setup: Asked why he wants to be president
jokes.punchline: Garrett's complete misunderstanding of the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Garrett confusion. Economical and absurd while staying true to character.

jokes.text: Face it, Annie. Politics are all about ego, popularity, and parlor tricks.
jokes.setup: Annie defending politics as about larger causes
jokes.punchline: Jeff's cynical but accurate assessment
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff cynicism that's both character-appropriate and thematically relevant. Good but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Well, if I see one, I won't.
jokes.punchline: Annie's comeback implying Jeff isn't really a lawyer
jokes.explanation: Solid comeback with good economy. Annie getting a dig in at Jeff's professional status.

jokes.text: I guess I noticed because I'm used to being the only observer.
jokes.setup: Secret Service asking how Abed noticed surveillance
jokes.punchline: Abed's self-aware comment about his observational nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed meta-commentary on his role as the show's observer. Character-perfect and self-aware without being forced.

jokes.text: Well, we're not used to being noticed. That sounds a little sad.
jokes.punchline: Secret Service agent's unexpectedly vulnerable moment
jokes.explanation: Humanizing moment for the Secret Service agent that subverts expectations. Good character work.

jokes.text: Although I could issue a warning for this bootleg of The Last Airbender. Where were you a week ago?
jokes.punchline: Joke about how bad The Last Airbender movie was, suggesting earlier intervention needed
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference joke about a widely panned movie. Decent timing but relies on external reference.

jokes.text: You can keep it.
jokes.punchline: Abed's deadpan agreement that the movie is worthless
jokes.explanation: Good Abed deadpan response that extends the joke economically.

jokes.text: I believe that humankind need not be governed. I don't care.
jokes.punchline: Jeff's blunt dismissal showing how little the process matters
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Jeff's dismissive attitude. The economy is excellent - maximum impact with minimal words.

jokes.text: I think 9/11 was bad. And freedom, well, I think that's just a little bit better.
jokes.punchline: Stating the most obvious political positions possible
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of empty political rhetoric. The understatement 'a little bit better' is perfect. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I'm Troy 'Butt Soup' Barnes. What? That's my name.
jokes.setup: Troy introducing himself as news anchor
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous middle name followed by defensive explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy absurdity. The defensive 'What? That's my name' sells it completely.

jokes.text: Leonard Rodriguez... Did he change his last name? Yeah, he's trying to court the Hispanic vote.
jokes.punchline: Leonard transparently pandering by changing his name
jokes.explanation: Good political pandering joke. Leonard doing this is appropriately desperate and cynical for community college politics.

jokes.text: It's like God spilled a person.
jokes.punchline: Poetic description of Garrett as a divine accident
jokes.explanation: Incredibly creative and economical insult. Poetic while being devastatingly funny. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Magnitude. Short for Magnetic Attitude. He's a one-man party.
jokes.punchline: The explanation of his name as 'Magnetic Attitude'
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay explanation for Magnitude's name. Fits his party-centric character well.

jokes.text: Do you just constantly have your own little side adventures? Yep. Me too.
jokes.punchline: Both acknowledging they're in side plots
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on subplot structure. Both characters acknowledging their narrative function.

jokes.text: Bring it on, Ponce de Leon. I'm gonna, Greg Muldunna. It's a real guy. He owns a mattress store downtown. You can look it up.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to deliver a tough comeback
jokes.punchline: Completely deflating with random mattress store owner reference
jokes.explanation: Masterful anti-climax. Jeff's comeback completely falls apart in the most specific, random way possible. The defensive 'you can look it up' is perfect.

jokes.text: I'm gonna eat your brains, Vicki. I'm gonna slurp 'em right out of that melon you call a head.
jokes.punchline: Absurdly violent threat in Pierce's old man voice
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce inappropriate aggression. The 'melon' detail makes it more absurd than actually threatening.

jokes.text: A reminder to all candidates: Your microphones are currently on.
jokes.punchline: Dean's matter-of-fact reminder that everyone heard Pierce's threats
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man moment from Dean. The timing and understated delivery makes Pierce's behavior funnier.

jokes.text: The assailant known only as "the asscrack bandit" will be brought to justice.
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous name 'asscrack bandit' treated as a serious threat
jokes.explanation: 'Asscrack bandit' is brilliantly absurd and highly quotable. Annie's serious tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: They want me to do what I'll say.
jokes.setup: Jeff's campaign speech about what he'll do
jokes.punchline: Word-scramble that sounds profound but means nothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect political double-talk that sounds meaningful but is just word salad. Classic Jeff manipulation.

jokes.text: Oh, they love it when you shuffle the words around.
jokes.punchline: Dean's awareness of the manipulation technique
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on political rhetoric techniques. Good observation but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Pop! Pop!
jokes.punchline: His signature catchphrase as entire political platform
jokes.explanation: Perfect Magnitude character consistency. Maximum economy with his entire political philosophy being 'Pop! Pop!'

jokes.text: I went with four. Geez, get a room, you two.
jokes.setup: Discussing duck-duck-goose strategy with Secret Service
jokes.punchline: Agent telling them to get a room for their nerdy bonding
jokes.explanation: Cute romantic subplot development through shared nerdiness. Character-appropriate bonding.

jokes.text: I didn't know how to use scissors, I sat in the middle of the seesaw, and I always found the distinction between duck and goose to be very arbitrary.
jokes.setup: Secret Service asking why Abed repeated first grade
jokes.punchline: His logical but socially oblivious childhood behaviors
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed backstory - each example shows his analytical mind missing social cues. The duck/goose observation is particularly brilliant.

jokes.text: I was only here to get back at her for not lending me a pencil.
jokes.setup: Pierce dropping out after Vicki withdraws
jokes.punchline: Revealing his entire political campaign was petty revenge over a pencil
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce pettiness. Entire political ambition reduced to pencil grudge. Character-perfect escalation.

jokes.text: My platform will be one high enough to push Vicki off to her death.
jokes.setup: Pierce asked about his political platform
jokes.punchline: His literal interpretation as murder weapon
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce darkness - taking political metaphor literally for violent ends

jokes.text: No matter what you're told... We have to clean the mold!
jokes.setup: Annie's campaign slogan about black mold
jokes.punchline: Catchy rhyming political slogan
jokes.explanation: Effective political slogan that's both catchy and about an actual issue. Good Annie earnestness.

jokes.text: Read my lips... No matter what you're told... We have to clean the mold.
jokes.setup: Annie turning her platform into a chant
jokes.punchline: The rhyming slogan referencing Bush's 'read my lips' promise
jokes.explanation: Good political parody with the Bush reference. The rhyme makes it catchy and campaign-appropriate.

jokes.text: We talked to two people at a vending machine. We don't even know how to do margins of error.
jokes.setup: Troy reporting poll results with 98% margin of error
jokes.punchline: Admitting their polling methodology is completely worthless
jokes.explanation: Good parody of news polling with Troy's honest admission of incompetence. The vending machine detail makes it funnier.

jokes.text: We talked to two people at a vending machine. Well, I'm being told we're taking a quick break, but stay tuned. They have to stay tuned. It's closed circuit television. Don't know what that means.
jokes.setup: Discussing their polling methodology and broadcast
jokes.punchline: Multiple escalating jokes about their amateur broadcast
jokes.explanation: Great meta-commentary on amateur broadcasting with escalating incompetence. Multiple laugh points.

jokes.text: Can I get an apple juice, please? Freshly squeezed this time or so help me God, Maurice.
jokes.punchline: Demanding fresh-squeezed apple juice with unnecessary threat
jokes.explanation: Good Pierce entitlement humor. The specific threat to Maurice adds character specificity.

jokes.text: That's close enough. Are you sure? You smell like nice soap.
jokes.punchline: Abed's awkward compliment about soap smell
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed awkward romance. The soap compliment is endearingly specific and weird.

jokes.text: I'm sorry you weren't a more obvious potential threat to the country.
jokes.punchline: Her backhanded romantic regret about his lack of criminality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant line that perfectly captures the absurd romantic situation - wishing someone was more dangerous so you could stay interested.

jokes.text: I fear a political career will shine a negative light on my drug dealing.
jokes.punchline: His casual admission that politics would hurt his drug business
jokes.explanation: Great subversion - instead of politics being corrupt, Starburns is too corrupt for politics. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I fear a political career will shine a negative light on my drug dealing.
jokes.punchline: Backwards priorities about career concerns
jokes.explanation: Perfect inverted priorities joke. Politics being bad for drug dealing is wonderfully backwards logic.

jokes.text: Mine's a three-way tie. Red, white, and blue.
jokes.punchline: Pandering patriotic answer disguised as personal preference
jokes.explanation: Smart political pandering joke. The 'three-way tie' setup makes the patriotic payoff funnier.

jokes.text: My presentation is a copy of Jeff Winger's 1997 audition tape for MTV's The Real World.
jokes.punchline: Nuclear option of embarrassing Jeff with old audition tape
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that promises maximum embarrassment. The specificity of MTV Real World makes it funnier.

jokes.text: ♪ Well I guess it would be nice if I could be on MTV ♪ ♪ so please please take a look at me ♪ ♪ for the real world ♪ ♪ Oh I'm gonna apply twice ♪
jokes.setup: Jeff's embarrassing audition tape being played
jokes.punchline: Cringey earnest song with terrible lyrics
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy. The earnest terribleness of the lyrics makes this devastatingly funny. High impact moment.

jokes.text: We've all been 19, Jeff, and none of us did this. None of us.
jokes.punchline: Annie's devastating dismissal of the age excuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect killer blow. Annie's repetition of 'None of us' drives home how uniquely embarrassing Jeff's tape is.

jokes.text: I hate you! I hate this school! And I hope you all get black mold poisoning!
jokes.punchline: Meltdown ending with spiteful reference to Annie's campaign issue
jokes.explanation: Good escalating meltdown that ties back to the main campaign issue. Character-appropriate tantrum.

jokes.text: When we seek to destroy others, we often hurt our self. Because it is the self that wants to be destroyed.
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly profound statement from Pierce
jokes.explanation: Subversive Pierce moment where he's actually wise. The philosophical depth is funny because it's Pierce.

jokes.text: Pierce, you're not usually so poignant. Well, Vicki finally lent me her pencil.
jokes.setup: Annie commenting on Pierce's unexpected wisdom
jokes.punchline: Pierce revealing his wisdom came from getting the pencil he wanted all along
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that explains Pierce's personality shift. The mundane reason for his wisdom is perfectly Pierce.

jokes.text: Well, Vicki finally lent me her pencil.
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's just because he got the pencil back
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce deflation. The profound moment immediately undercut by petty pencil resolution. Great callback structure.

jokes.text: Two men fighting for the same piece of earth. One recently born. One soon to die. A competition reflecting the pointlessness of life.
jokes.setup: Abed describing the Magnitude vs Leonard matchup
jokes.punchline: His overly dramatic and existential description of a community college election
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Abed moment treating trivial events with cinematic gravitas. The life/death framing of a student election is perfect.

jokes.text: Pull up, Abed. You're in a nosedive.
jokes.setup: Abed getting overly existential about the election
jokes.punchline: Troy's aviation metaphor for Abed's spiral into pretension
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy line - he understands Abed well enough to recognize when he's getting too deep. The aviation metaphor is inspired.

jokes.text: South Park.
jokes.setup: Announcing election winner with dramatic pause
jokes.punchline: Cartoon show won the election
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd ending. Maximum economy with just two words delivering a complete rejection of the political process.

jokes.text: Did you know you could make napalm out of common dish soap and cat food?
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of bomb-making information
jokes.explanation: Dark Abed humor that explains why Secret Service is concerned. Casually delivered dangerous information.

jokes.text: I had a dream I was the regular president.
jokes.punchline: Garrett's modest presidential dream
jokes.explanation: Sweet Garrett moment. The distinction between 'regular' and student president is endearingly specific.

jokes.text: Don't call me Kickpuncher. Call me...David.
jokes.punchline: Superhero wanting to be called by normal name
jokes.explanation: Cute Troy fantasy moment. The anti-climactic 'David' provides a gentle laugh.

jokes.text: Cool. Cool, cool, cool.
jokes.setup: Abed getting positive response from agent
jokes.punchline: His signature verbal tic
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed catchphrase. Low impact but character-consistent ending to his subplot.

tmdb_overview: For an upcoming vice-presidential visit, Glendale's Dean Pelton hastily organizes an election for student body president that pits Annie against Jeff.
insight: Campaign chaos sustains 68 jokes despite middling impact scores across the hour.
